title: Welcome Screen — Basic Output
description: Write a C++ program that displays a welcome screen for a text adventure game. Show the game title, a short intro paragraph, and a description of the starting room (e.g., 'You are standing in a dark forest clearing.'). Use cout with formatting like endl, \t, and \n to make the output readable. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
test_code:
assert '#include <iostream>' in cpp, 'Must include <iostream> for input/output'
assert 'cout' in cpp, 'Use cout to display the welcome screen and room description'
assert 'endl' in cpp or '\\n' in cpp, 'Use endl or \\n to format output across multiple lines'
assert cpp.count('cout') >= 3, 'Use at least 3 cout statements (title, intro, room description)'
assert 'return 0' in cpp, 'Program should return 0 from main'
assert 'main()' in cpp or 'main ()' in cpp, 'Program needs a main function'
acceptance_criteria: Game title is displayed using cout
An intro paragraph describes the game premise
A starting room description is printed
Output uses endl or \n for line breaks
At least 3 cout statements for distinct sections
hints: Use cout << "==============================" << endl; to create visual separators
Use \t for tab indentation to make descriptions stand out
Think of a theme: dark forest, haunted castle, space station, etc.
The room description should mention what the player can see and possible directions to go
